Topic 1: Iran overview (no negotiations & ground operations)

[174:40]based on rumors, I guess, that Trump was about to announce an imminent end to the war in Iran.

[176:41]The press office, the secretary of state, the secretary of war, Trump himself, various

[176:52]All of this is being put out there to effectively saturate the information space.

[177:01]And they don't want Iran to know their next move.

Topic 3: Part 1 (this week's recap: performative peace, changing goals & Trump's empty speech)

[187:11]It will be five full weeks of war in Iran on Friday.

[187:17]And there is no sign of the war ending.

[187:20]As you know, we are in a stalemate.

[187:22]The price of oil is exploding.

[187:25]And even after Trump's speech tonight, if the goal was to reassure the markets, it had

[187:30]the opposite effect.

[187:32]And I imagine the reason for this is because the market believed the hype.

[187:38]They thought that the speech would announce an imminent end to the fighting.

[187:45]They priced in that perhaps the oil market would normalize or begin to normalize starting

[187:52]immediately.

[187:53]And when Trump did not announce that, I think this is why after the speech, oil prices shot

[187:59]right back up in the futures market, and now oil is up to $116 a barrel, I think.

[188:06]So it's steadily climbing Iran although they are not launching as many projectiles as before

[188:13]They are still launching missiles and drones at the Gulf States at Israel

[188:18]They're still targeting commercial shipping and the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz

[188:22]So everything is basically as it was everything is still the way it has been for the past several weeks

[188:30]The big development from tonight, as you know, is the primetime address by President Trump earlier

[188:36]tonight at, I believe, nine o'clock Eastern time is when it broadcasts. It was about 20 minutes.

[188:43]And I'll just refresh your memory a little bit. Where we have been for the past 48 hours

[188:50]is there has been, it seems, a concerted messaging campaign, I think originating from the White House.

[188:58]trying to convince people that the president is leaning towards ending the conflict immediately and

[189:06]This would be a shock

[189:07]This would really be a pivot because when Trump announced the conflict initially he articulated five major goals

[189:15]Almost none of which have been achieved

[189:41]We have not suppressed their ability

[189:52]Oh, the regional proxies.

[189:54]We've done nothing to the proxies at all.

[189:57]and the Iranian regime remains intact.

[190:18]and this is now the central and overriding one.

[196:57]This is the function of rhetoric from the White House.

[197:01]This is how Trump sees it from a doctrinal doctrinal point of view.

[197:12]you saturate the information space

Topic 4: Part 2 (Iran's upper hand, offloading the fight & the oil crisis)

[197:48]What is the problem here?

[197:50]As we've talked about, we are stuck.

[197:53]We made an attempt to destroy the Iranian regime and we failed.

[197:59]We took a shot and we missed.

[198:02]And what this is allowed Iran to do is seize the Strait of Hormuz and take a fifth of the world's energy hostage, and we have no ability to take it back.

[198:12]Because the regime survived, it is now actually stronger. So it's not going anywhere.

[198:18]And what's more, Iran prepared for exactly this scenario. They prepared for a decapitation.

[198:25]And that is why not only do they have a very deep bench, which is...

[198:31]Thoroughly integrated in Iranian society, and that's the Iranian Revolutionary Guard,

[198:36]but it's also a decentralized command.

[198:40]So, they actually don't need their command control and communications, which have been

[198:45]neutralized by Israel and the United States, because they have delegated out to IRGC lieutenants

[198:52]and commanders their instructions.

[198:55]And that is why throughout this massive country, you have very small, very mobile, very abundant

[199:06]and they're operated by freelancing members of the IRGC.

[199:10]That is why for the past five weeks,

[199:12]drones and missiles are being launched all the time

[199:15]because they're not necessarily getting communication

[199:18]from the center.

[199:20]They don't need it.

[199:22]So Iran is prepared for exactly this eventuality.

[199:26]They survived the initial strike,

[199:29]which is all they needed to do.

[199:31]The regime is not toppled and actually now it's fortified because there's a rally around

[199:37]the flag effect.

[199:38]And that goes not just for the public, which supports the regime, which is under attack,

[199:43]but also for the regime itself.

[199:45]There is no factionalism.

[199:48]There doesn't seem to be a lot of cracks.

[199:51]They're sticking together and they're still able to attack the Persian Gulf, the Gulf

[199:59]So we're trapped in this situation as we've discussed where it seems that the only way

[200:04]out is either a complete surrender and that involves giving control of the straight to

[200:10]Iran permanently and formally, which is unacceptable, or we have to escalate.

[200:16]But Iran and we and everyone knows that we have no ability to escalate.

[200:28]And the way this is set up is that if that happens, we may win, but we have to give it

[200:34]our all and a lot of people are going to die.

[200:38]It's actually a question whether the United States could ever prevail against Iran short

[200:43]of using nuclear weapons.

[200:45]Could we even succeed if we did an Iraq level invasion of Iran?
